question:
Will Bayern München win on 2025-11-29?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for November 29, 2025
If Bayern München wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.bundesliga.com/en/bundesliga

evidences.title: Stoppage Time Rules in Soccer
evidences.content: injury in stoppage time. Stoppage time is often a source of contention, especially when one team's trailing or there's a contentious situation that unfolds late in the game. However, the decision lies solely with the referee who must consider fairness and the flow of the match. It is not an opportunity to extend the game artificially. By the way, I'm Dylan Joseph, trainer and number one bestselling Amazon author. Now, let's clarify some misconceptions about what is stoppage time. Stoppage time is not a fixed duration. A minimum of 1 minute is given, but often time it's much longer, especially in higher stakes games. The fourth official usually signals to the head referee what the stoppage time should be, who then signals to the players and to the fans. evidences.title: Champions League winner odds: Tight at the Top | Goal.com US
evidences.content: ### What Is Implied Probability? Implied probability converts betting odds into a percentage. It tells you exactly how likely the bookmakers believe an outcome is to happen. The simplest formula to calculate this uses decimal odds: 1 ÷ Decimal Odds × 100 = Implied Probability % Let's look at Arsenal’s current odds of 3.25: 1 ÷ 3.25 = 0.3076 0.3076 × 100 = 30.8% In plain English, this means bookies believe Arsenal has a roughly 30.8% chance of winning the Champions League. If you think Arsenal’s actual chance of winning is higher than 30.8%, that bet represents good "value." If you think their chances are lower, it's a bad bet. ### Why Do Odds Change? Futures odds are a live, breathing market. They fluctuate constantly based on several variables: [...] | Team | Odds | Implied % Probability | --- | Bayern Munich | +200 | 33.3 | | Paris Saint-Germain | +225 | 33.3 | | Arsenal | +225 | 30.8 | | Atletico Madrid | +800 | 11.1 | Odds courtesy of US Sportsbook. evidences.title: Added time in soccer: rules, regulations & records | World Football FAQ | Bundesliga
evidences.content: How long is added time in soccer? The length of injury time in soccer varies from game to game, depending on the number of stoppages and the referee’s decision. The rules don’t specify how long added time should last and there is no maximum stoppage time in soccer. It all works on a game-by-game basis. In the official rules, there are various reasons as to why time is added in soccer. These include: [...] Soccer is simple enough. There are two teams, the ball is round, and the match lasts for 90 minutes... or does it? According to Spanish newspaper Marca, the ball is in play for around only 50 minutes per game. The rest of the time is taken up by interruptions, such as substitutions, corners, throw-ins, goal kicks, fouls, and free kicks. This missing time is compensated in soccer at the end of each half with what’s known as “added time”, “injury time”, or “stoppage time”. In this article, you’ll find everything you need to know about the rule, including what it is, how long it is, and records through the ages. How long is added time in soccer? [...] Who decides on the added time in soccer? During the game, the referee keeps track of any stoppages in play. At the end of each half...

evidences.title: FIFA International Match Calendar - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Association football scheduling agreement The FIFA International Match Calendar (sometimes abbreviated as the FIFA Calendar) is an outline agreement between FIFA, the six continental association football confederations, the European Club Association, and FIFPro, which sets out which dates can be used for "official" and "friendly" international matches. Individual periods of these dates are commonly referred to as "international breaks". FIFA insists that official and friendly matches within designated breaks take precedence over domestic matches, so clubs are required to release players to go play with their national teams, while international friendlies that take place outside the designated dates do not take precedence, and clubs may retain players. [...] Official matches have a release period of four days, which means that players can take up to four days away from club duties to partake in national team duties. Because most breaks include 2-3 games, players take at least 8-12 days away from their clubs. If a player participates in an official match on a different continent from their club's, the release period is five days. Friendly matches are deemed less important and the...

evidences.title: Weather Delay / Cancellation Timeline
evidences.content: ### Shortening a Match To make every effort to play the match, referees will use the below timeline to determine when to enter a delay or to cancel the match for the evening. The match will be delayed 30 minutes from the last visible lightning or audible thunderclap, with the latest possible restart being defined as the time when the equivalent of one half of the game time could be played. ### Timeline #### 11v11 Matches An 11v11 match consists of 80 minutes of game play, thus 40 minutes are required to play (2 20 minute halves, plus a 5 minute halftime). Below are the delay/cancellation guidelines based on schedule match start time: [...] Skip to content English Sign in [![FXA Adult Sports Ball Gif small.gif]]( Main Site 1. Help Center 2. Rules 3. ⚽ Soccer # Weather Delay / Cancellation Timeline In the event of weather or other outside impact, a match may be shortened to allow for completion during the originally scheduled match dates. A game is considered complete if one full half is played, and will not be rescheduled. If a match doesn't begin, or must be halted in the first half, and isn't resumed, it will be rescheduled. ### Shortening a Match [...] 8pm - cancelled if light...

evidences.content: Soccer image Soccer image ... Fantasy image Fantasy image Watch image Watch image Network image Network image USA TODAY Sports USA TODAY Sports # What is stoppage time? Know the rule for 2026 World Cup Video Player Cover There are several rules of soccer to be aware of for the 2026 World Cup. One of the unique factors of the beautiful game is the flow of the match. This is in part created by the fact that there is a running clock, which doesn't stop when the ball goes out of bounds, if there's an injury, a substitution, or any other situation where the time would be stopped in another sport. This might be odd to fans of the NBA where the last two minutes of a game can take a half hour, but once someone gets the hang of the idea of stoppage time, it's easy to settle into the action. evidences.title: Law 7 - The Duration of the Match
evidences.content: Players are entitled to an interval at half-time, not exceeding 15 minutes; a short drinks break (which should not exceed one minute) is permitted at the interval of half-time in extra time. Competition rules must state the duration of the half-time interval and it may be altered only with the referee’s permission. 3. Allowance for time lost The referee must not compensate for a timekeeping error during the first half by changing the length of the second half. 4. Penalty kick If a penalty kick has to be taken or retaken, the half is extended until the penalty kick is completed. 5. Abandoned match An abandoned match is replayed unless the competition rules or organisers determine otherwise. ## Useful Resources The FA ## The FA Handbook The FA ## Law 14: The Penalty Kick The FA [...] The FA Manchester City seal FA Cup glory FA Cup Final stats: Chelsea v Manchester City 2024-28 women's and girls' strategy released Women's and girls' strategy launch explained PGMOL appointments policy Football & Me: South Asian Heritage Month All Together - UK FIFA Women's World Cup 2035 bid EDI Calendar 2025-26 FULL-TIME offers you an easy way to manage your football leagues online. evidences.content: FIFA # International Match Calendars After consultation with the relevant stakeholders, FIFA publishes the International Match Calendar (IMC) for the period of four or eight years. FIFA’s objective in this process is to make a football calendar that is “fit-for-purpose” for modern needs, contributing for the improvement of the quality of players, clubs and national teams and giving both incentive and opportunity for the detection and development of talent outside a small number of countries.

evidences.content: can be estimated automatically by comparing successive composites. The prediction of the most recent composite is based on the previously estimated movement of precipitation as a purely advective process. Dynamic processes such as amplification and weakening are not taken into account. Therefore, the quality of the forecast typically decreases strongly with increasing forecast time, thus complementing the numerical weather forecast especially in the first one to two hours. The strength of such a nowcasting approach lies in its rapid availability at a high update frequency. Starting from the most up-to-date analysis composite, a seamless areal forecast of the short-term development of precipi- tation is thus made possible. The methods for composite generation and displacement are applied [...] Summary With SINFONY (Seamless INtegrated FOrecastiNg sYstem), DWD has been developing a new fore-casting system since 2017, which will enable a seamless forecast of weather events with damage potential and their probability of occurrence for short-range forecasting (0 to 12 hours). To do this, the existing Nowcasting (NWC) and Numerical Weather Prediction (NWP) methods are being improved a...
